jobPostingInfo.title: 2026 Fall: Transmission System Planning Co-op
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
Eversource will not offer immigration-related sponsorship for this position. Applicants who require immigration sponsorship—either now or in the future—should not apply. This includes, but is not limited to, direct company sponsorship, listing Eversource as the employer of record on immigration documents, or any work authorization that requires company involvement or documentation (e.g., H-1B, OPT, STEM OPT, CPT, TN, J-1, O-1, etc.).

2026 Fall Transmission System Planning Co-op

Kickstart your career with hands-on experience at New England’s largest electric utility. As an Eversource Engineering Intern, you’ll gain real-world exposure to the energy industry while receiving professional development, coaching, and training. Interns are valued team members, contributing fresh perspectives and innovative solutions across a variety of projects.

The Eversource Intern & Co-op program is designed to energize you with hands-on learning, leadership exposure, and real-world impact.

What You’ll Get:

Practical experience in a dynamic work environment
Career development and mentorship
Exposure to hands-on engineering tasks and experienced teams
Opportunities available in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire


Join us and power your future with Eversource!

Position Summary:
The Transmission System Planning Co-op will support the development and analysis of electric transmission system models and assist in evaluating system performance under various operating conditions. This role is designed for students or recent graduates with foundational knowledge in power systems and electrical engineering who are eager to gain hands-on experience in utility planning, operations support, and reliability studies.

Essential Functions:

Model Development Support:

  • Assist in building and maintaining transmission system models used for planning studies.
  • Collect and review forecasted load data for inclusion in system models.
  • Help gather and validate system parameters such as equipment ratings, impedances, protection settings, and network topology.

Study Assistance:

  • Support transmission system impact studies across Eversource’s service territories.
  • Contribute to the development of study scopes and documentation under guidance.
  • Apply planning standards and regulatory criteria (e.g., NERC, NPCC, ISO-NE) in study work.
  • Participate in testing solution alternatives to address identified system issues.

Technical Analysis:

  • Assist in performing steady-state, stability, short circuit, and electromagnetic transient (EMT) analyses.
  • Support System Operations via researching disturbance events and other analyses.
  • Help automate study processes using transmission analysis tools.
  • Interface with internal departments and external entities

Collaboration & Communication:

  • Work closely with senior engineers and cross-functional teams.
  • Interface with internal departments and external entities such as ISO-NE.
  • Support documentation of study results and preparation of technical reports.
  • Contribute to the development of training materials and presentations.

Technical Knowledge/Skills:

  • Basic understanding of power system concepts including modeling, load flow, and symmetrical components.
  • Exposure to power system analysis tools such as PSSE, ASPEN, PSCAD, or similar software is a plus.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Team-oriented with effective interpersonal and organizational skills.
  • Eagerness to learn and take initiative in a technical environment.

Education:

  • Currently pursuing a B.S. in Electrical Engineering, preferably with a focus on Electric Power Systems.

Experience:

  • Coursework or project experience related to transmission planning, power system analysis, or electrical design preferred.

Interns are paid through our Managed Service Provider Program and are not employees of Eversource. Pay rates are hourly, based on year of graduation and if the role is technical or non-technical. Rates start at $20.60 for a freshman and go up to $31.35 for graduate level students.

Emergency Response:

Responding to emergency situations to meet customers’ needs is part of every employee’s role. If employed, you will be given an Emergency Restoration assignment. This means you may be called to assist during an emergency outside of your normal responsibilities, work hours and location.

EEO Statement:

Eversource Energy is an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, religion, disability status, or protected veteran status.
